title: One-to-Many Relationships Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on One-to-Many database relationships.

questions.question: Which of these is a perfect example of a One-to-Many (1:N) relationship?
options: A Book and its single publisher
A Library and its single building

questions.question: On which 'side' of the relationship should the foreign key be defined?
In a 1:N relationship between Library and Book, where would the 'library_id' most likely be stored?
In Sequelize, which association method would you use on the parent model (the 'one' side)?
Which of these is true about one-to-many relationships in NoSQL databases?
options: They can be modeled by embedding child documents within a parent document
They must use foreign keys like SQL
They are only modeled with external APIs
